launch_cutline.description: A marketed M5 surface ends the milestone XT-qualified (at or above Stable) only when every certification cell -- onboarding/migration, first-useful-work, command discoverability, component-state and appearance parity, durable attention, notification privacy, embedded boundary, desktop conformance, and accessibility/i18n -- is current on the claimed profile, the owner is signed off, no forbidden v1-shell dependency is present, and the family register publishes it at or above Stable. A surface that loses any of those narrows to Beta or holds at Preview rather than inheriting an adjacent green surface.

stop_rules.title: Certification evidence is stale on the claimed profile
stop_rules.rationale: A surface whose XT evidence breached its freshness SLO cannot keep a Stable claim.

stop_rules.title: Certification evidence is missing on the claimed profile
stop_rules.rationale: A surface missing a required XT evidence cell must hold below the cutline.

stop_rules.title: Owner sign-off is absent
stop_rules.rationale: A surface without owner sign-off cannot carry a Stable XT claim.

stop_rules.title: Certification is policy-blocked on the deployment row
stop_rules.rationale: A managed-deployment surface blocked by policy must hold below the cutline.

stop_rules.title: Hidden onboarding/setup gap
stop_rules.rationale: No marketed surface may widen while its onboarding/setup work is hidden or unproven.

stop_rules.title: Embedded high-risk approval path
stop_rules.rationale: A surface that approves high-risk actions inside an embedded pane must hold; auth belongs in the system browser.

stop_rules.title: Toast-only long-running truth
stop_rules.rationale: Long-running truth must survive in a durable surface, not a dismissible toast.

stop_rules.title: Theme-only state semantics
stop_rules.rationale: State meaning may never depend on color/theme alone; it must carry a non-theme signal.

publication.rationale: Widening is held: at least one release-blocking marketed surface is not XT-qualified. Each blocked surface lists the dimension(s) and reason(s) holding it below the cutline.
